intro: Wednesday brings Google's A2A partner fund first-cohort announcement — 47 companies sharing $750M — and Microsoft's Q1 2026 earnings preview showing Copilot revenue has crossed $10B annualised for the first time. Meta releases Llama 4 benchmark data confirming Maverick is within 8% of GPT-5.5 on reasoning tasks at open-weight pricing. India's Sarvam AI begins its multilingual model training run.

top_stories.headline: Google A2A Partner Fund First Cohort: 47 Companies, $750M, Salesforce and ServiceNow Among Recipients
top_stories.summary: Google announced the first cohort of its A2A partner fund — 47 companies receiving between $5M and $25M each for A2A-native agent development, totalling approximately $680M of the committed $750M. Recipients include Salesforce (Agentforce A2A integration), ServiceNow (AI workflow agents), Zendesk (support automation), Workday (HR and finance agents), and 43 smaller enterprise AI startups. Each recipient must ship an A2A-native product within 180 days or return the funding. Google said it received over 1,200 applications in 10 days — a 25× oversubscription rate.

top_stories.headline: Microsoft Q1 2026 Earnings Preview: Copilot ARR Crosses $10B for First Time, Azure AI Grows 78%
top_stories.summary: Ahead of Microsoft's Q1 2026 earnings release, analyst previews indicate Copilot has crossed $10B in annualised revenue — the first enterprise AI product from any company to reach this milestone outside of Anthropic and OpenAI. Azure AI services revenue grew approximately 78% year-over-year in Q1, driven by GPT-5.5 adoption and Copilot for Security's rapid enterprise take-up. Microsoft's AI revenue is now estimated to represent 18% of total company revenue, up from 11% a year ago.

top_stories.headline: Meta Releases Llama 4 Maverick Benchmarks — Within 8% of GPT-5.5 on Reasoning at Open-Weight Pricing
top_stories.summary: Meta published comprehensive Llama 4 Maverick benchmark data showing the model performs within 8% of GPT-5.5 on multi-step reasoning tasks, within 5% on code generation, and exceeds GPT-5.5 on multilingual tasks across 12 languages. The benchmarks position Maverick as the first open-weight model to directly compete with GPT-5.5 on its strongest capabilities — at API pricing that is approximately one-fifth of GPT-5.5 standard tier. Meta simultaneously opened Maverick fine-tuning via Meta AI Studio to all enterprise customers.

top_stories.headline: EU AI Act First Enforcement Actions: Three Companies Under Investigation for Prohibited-Use Violations
top_stories.summary: The EU AI Office confirmed it has opened formal investigations into three companies — two retail chains for biometric categorisation of customers and one municipal government for a social scoring system — within 24 hours of the EU AI Act's prohibited-use enforcement beginning. None of the companies have been fined yet; investigations typically take 3-6 months. However, the immediate action signals that the EU AI Office is prepared to enforce, reversing expectations that enforcement would be slow to start.

top_stories.headline: Perplexity Computer Agent Adds Memory Layer — Persistent Enterprise Context Across Sessions
top_stories.summary: Perplexity launched a memory layer for its Computer agent product — allowing enterprise users to maintain persistent context across research sessions, with the agent remembering past queries, preferred sources, and organisation-specific knowledge. The feature directly addresses enterprise buyers' primary objection to AI research agents: that each session starts from scratch. Perplexity said early enterprise pilots with the memory layer show a 40% increase in session depth and a 65% increase in daily active usage among research teams.

quick_hits.text: OpenAI confirmed that GPT-5.5 Pro is now available to all ChatGPT Pro subscribers ($200/month) without usage caps — the first model tier where Pro subscribers have uncapped access to a frontier agentic model.

Nvidia's H200 NVL multi-cloud launch drove its data centre revenue forecast upgrade — analysts now project Nvidia data centre revenue at $48B for Q1 2026, up from $42B prior estimate, as hyperscaler H200 orders accelerate.

India's BharatGen project (IIT Bombay) began pre-training its Hindi-first foundation model on its IndiaAI Mission compute allocation — targeting 30B parameter scale with full Apache 2.0 release planned for Q4 2026.

DeepSeek V4 self-hosted deployments now account for an estimated 40% of all V4 inference globally — surpassing the official DeepSeek API in usage volume just five days after release, driven by enterprises optimising for sub-$0.50/M token costs.

Anthropic Claude Enterprise 3.0 early access applications closed with over 800 enterprise sign-ups in the first 48 hours — Anthropic is prioritising healthcare and financial services customers for initial onboarding.

tools_products.name: Perplexity Computer Agent with Memory
tools_products.what: Persistent enterprise context layer for the Computer agent — remembers past queries, preferred sources, and org-specific knowledge across sessions, increasing daily active usage 65% in pilots.

tools_products.name: Llama 4 Maverick Fine-Tuning
tools_products.what: Enterprise fine-tuning for Llama 4 Maverick now open in Meta AI Studio — within 8% of GPT-5.5 on reasoning, with API pricing at approximately one-fifth of GPT-5.5 standard.

tools_products.name: Microsoft Copilot ($10B ARR)
tools_products.what: First enterprise AI product outside OpenAI/Anthropic to cross $10B ARR — driven by Azure AI integration and Copilot for Security's 10,000-customer first-week adoption.

tools_products.name: GPT-5.5 Pro Uncapped for ChatGPT Pro
tools_products.what: GPT-5.5 Pro now available without usage caps to all ChatGPT Pro subscribers ($200/month) — the first uncapped frontier agentic model access at a consumer subscription price.

tools_products.name: EU AI Act Compliance Dashboard
tools_products.what: Official EU AI Office portal for filing compliance documentation, checking prohibited-use classifications, and tracking investigation status — the primary tool for enterprises managing EU AI Act exposure.

india_roundup.text: IIT Bombay's BharatGen began pre-training its Hindi-first foundation model at 30B parameter scale on IndiaAI Mission GPU allocation — targeting Apache 2.0 release in Q4 2026 as India's first fully open sovereign foundation model.

Sarvam AI's 22-language multilingual model training run is underway on NVIDIA H100 infrastructure — the company has committed to releasing benchmark results by end of May, with full model access planned for enterprise customers by August 2026.

India's GCC count crossed 1,800 active centres in April 2026 — with AI-focused GCCs representing 23% of new GCC setups, the highest share ever recorded, as global enterprises accelerate India-based AI development teams.

PhonePe's Krutrim-based UPI fraud detection processed its first billion transactions with AI triage — detecting and blocking 94.3% of fraud attempts before transaction completion across 500M+ daily payment events.

India's DPDP Act rulemaking consultation period closes May 15 — AI companies with Indian operations are submitting comments through NASSCOM, with data localisation and consent management requirements the most contested provisions.

closing_insight: Microsoft Copilot crossing $10B ARR is the proof-of-concept that enterprise AI monetisation at scale is real — not theoretical — and it is happening faster than every analyst model predicted twelve months ago.
